primary_muscles.description: Muscle ids this exercise trains as a primary target

force.description: Descriptive metadata only — nothing in the engine consumes it. The plan critique balances push/pull by MUSCLE GROUP (chest/front-delts/triceps vs upper-back/lats/rear-delts/biceps), which is the right model for hypertrophy volume; do not wire this field into that check without revisiting the ambiguous cases (lateral raises, abs, hip abduction).

resistance_profile.description: Where in the ROM the exercise is hardest, e.g. 'hardest in the lengthened position'

lengthened_bias.description: Loads the stretched/lengthened position heavily. Kept for the plan engine (which reads this boolean); MUST equal (loading_bias === 'lengthened') when loading_bias is present.

loading_bias.description: Where in the range the exercise challenges the muscle most: 'lengthened' (hardest at the stretch — e.g. incline curl, RDL), 'shortened' (hardest at peak contraction — e.g. leg extension, cable crossover), 'mid-range' (hardest mid-way — most free-weight compounds), or 'uniform' (cables/machines with an even profile). The reader-facing, richer version of lengthened_bias.

cns_cost.description: Systemic / central-nervous-system fatigue cost per hard set. Heavy multi-joint barbell lifts (squat, deadlift) are 'high'; most machine/cable isolations are 'low'. Informs how many can be programmed together and recovery between sessions.

execution_steps.description: Ordered step-by-step guide to performing the exercise from setup to finish — the 'how do I do this?' walkthrough a novice can follow.

good_when.description: Situations where this is a strong pick (e.g. 'limited equipment', 'want a deep loaded stretch', 'lower-back-friendly quad work').

bad_when.description: Situations where a different exercise is better (e.g. 'cranky lower back', 'no way to progress load', 'shoulder impingement').

progressions.description: How to make it harder (add load, reps, ROM, tempo, or a harder variation).

regressions.description: How to make it easier (less load/ROM, a supported or assisted variation).
